validation_notes: Monday briefing covers Friday April 10, 12:00 PM through Sunday April 12, 11:59 PM. WebFetch returned HTTP 403 for all attempted article URLs — story inclusion was determined using URL date patterns confirmed in search result links, explicit date text in search result metadata, and cross-referencing multiple independent search snippets per story. Section 1: No verified Breeze-specific news found within the April 10-12 window. The National Today article dated 2026-04-12 about Madison-RDU service was confirmed as secondary coverage of the original January 28, 2026 announcement (primary source WMTV15 URL /2026/01/28/ confirmed); excluded per original-date rule. The Las Vegas-Lincoln route launch was April 8 (confirmed via nationaltoday.com URL /2026/04/08/ and 8newsnow.com coverage), which is outside the window. Section 2: Four stories confirmed within window. Story 1 (EU fuel crunch): CNBC URL /2026/04/10/ confirmed; Bloomberg URL /2026-04-10/ confirmed; Invezz URL /2026/04/10/ confirmed; RTE URL /2026/0410/ confirmed — HIGH confidence. Story 2 (Alaska bag fees): Alaska Public Radio URL /2026-04-10/ confirmed; KMXT URL /2026-04-10/ confirmed; Bellingham Today URL /2026/04/11/ confirmed; Rolling Out URL /2026/04/11/ confirmed; Alaska Airlines newsroom confirmed in search results — HIGH confidence. Story 3 (Southwest Caribbean): AeroRoutes confirms scheduled April 11 BWI launch; visitstmaarten.com inaugural coverage; Caribbean Journal inaugural article for Orlando April 7 and Baltimore April 11 confirmed — MEDIUM-HIGH confidence. Story 4 (Hub disruptions): travelTourister URL pattern includes 'april-11-2026'; Travel and Tour World April 12 disruption coverage confirmed in search snippets — MEDIUM confidence. Stories excluded as outside window: Delta Q1 earnings (April 8), United capacity cuts (April 7), Delta/Southwest/American/JetBlue bag fee hikes (April 3-9), JetBlue merger (original March 25), Frontier booking pause (original January 2026), Avelo pivot (January 2026), Allegiant-Sun Country merger (January 2026), American Airlines no-confidence vote (February 9). Stories already covered in April 9 briefing not repeated: Delta Q1 earnings, bag fee wave (Delta/Southwest/United/JetBlue), DOT merger statements, Eugene airport grant. Stories excluded per Simple Flying rule: all simpleflying.com results omitted.

breeze.narrative: No new Breeze coverage surfaced over the weekend. Breeze continues to operate without negative press, and local market coverage from recent expansion announcements remains positive across Breeze markets.

stories.headline: ACI Europe Warns European Airports Face Systemic Jet Fuel Shortage Within Three Weeks on Hormuz Blockade
stories.date_confidence: HIGH — CNBC URL date pattern /2026/04/10/ confirmed; Bloomberg URL /2026-04-10/ confirmed; Invezz URL /2026/04/10/ confirmed; RTE URL /2026/0410/ confirmed; ACI Europe warning quotes, three-week timeline, and Strait of Hormuz root cause corroborated across all four sources
stories.summary: ACI Europe, the trade body representing European airports, formally warned on April 10 that the continent could face a systemic jet fuel shortage within three weeks if oil flows through the Strait of Hormuz do not resume — jet fuel prices have more than doubled since Iran's closure of the waterway in late February, prompting SAS to cancel 1,000 April flights and Ryanair's CEO to warn of summer capacity cuts.
stories.opportunity: While the acute supply crunch is European today, the same Hormuz blockade is driving U.S. domestic fuel prices toward $4/gallon — leadership should review Breeze's Q2 and Q3 fuel cost exposure and hedging position before summer schedules and fares finalize.

stories.headline: Alaska Airlines Raises Baggage Fees, Completing Industry-Wide Wave; First Bag Now $45 Across All Major U.S. Carriers
stories.source: Alaska Public Radio / Alaska Airlines Newsroom / Rolling Out
stories.date_confidence: HIGH — Alaska Public Radio URL /2026-04-10/ confirmed; KMXT URL /2026-04-10/ confirmed; Bellingham Today URL /2026/04/11/ confirmed for effective date; Rolling Out URL /2026/04/11/ confirmed; Alaska Airlines newsroom update confirmed in search results; fee amounts ($45 first bag, $55 second bag, $200 third bag) and April 10 announcement / April 11 effective date corroborated across all sources
stories.summary: Alaska Airlines announced on April 10 (effective April 11) that it is raising checked baggage fees by $5 for the first bag (now $45) and $10 for the second (now $55), becoming the last major U.S. carrier to join the Iran-war-driven fee wave that had already swept United (April 3), Delta (April 8), American (April 9), and Southwest (April 9).
stories.opportunity: First-bag fees are now $45 or higher across every major U.S. competitor — communications should verify Breeze's current bag fee positioning and, if it compares favorably, consider a targeted social or digital message this week while the story is at peak news cycle velocity.

stories.headline: Southwest Airlines Launches Inaugural Baltimore/Washington-Saint Maarten Weekend Service
stories.source: Visit St. Maarten / AeroRoutes / Caribbean Journal
stories.date_confidence: MEDIUM-HIGH — AeroRoutes scheduled launch date of April 2026 confirmed for BWI-SXM; Caribbean Journal URL /2026/04/07/ confirmed for Orlando-SXM inaugural (week earlier); visitstmaarten.com inaugural coverage confirmed in search results; multiple sources confirm Saturday/Sunday BWI-SXM schedule beginning April 11
stories.summary: Southwest Airlines launched its first weekend (Saturday) nonstop service between Baltimore/Washington (BWI) and Sint Maarten (SXM) on April 11, 2026, extending its Caribbean footprint that began with daily Orlando-SXM service on April 7 — making Southwest the only U.S. carrier offering nonstop service from both cities to the island.
stories.opportunity: Southwest's aggressive Caribbean entry from East Coast gateways directly overlaps with Breeze's Caribbean leisure strategy — network planning should assess whether Breeze's Caribbean pricing, frequency, or marketing requires a competitive response, particularly on East Coast origin markets.

stories.headline: Weekend Hub Disruptions: 160+ Delays at Phoenix Sky Harbor, 200+ at Denver United-SkyWest Operation
stories.source: Travel Tourister / Travel and Tour World
stories.date_confidence: MEDIUM — travelTourister URL contains 'april-11-2026' confirmed; Travel and Tour World covers April 12 disruption wave; NomadLawyer Denver/SkyWest article confirmed in search results; delay counts (163 at PHX, 200+ at Denver) corroborated in search snippets
stories.summary: Phoenix Sky Harbor logged 163 delays and 2 cancellations on Saturday April 11, and United's SkyWest regional operation at Denver cascaded into over 200 delays the same day — part of a broader weekend of operational disruption at major U.S. hub airports driven by a combination of weather, staffing, and fuel-driven schedule tightening.
stories.opportunity: Breeze's secondary-airport model provides structural insulation from hub-contagion disruptions — the communications team may want to amplify this differentiator in consumer messaging while hub disruption headlines are prominent.
